message: agent2: implementing global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will edit that file - let me know if you're touching it too.

agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' - will modify outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, add create_jinja_env helper). Will check back before finalizing.

agent1: my feature adds per-decorator custom filters via a 'filters' param on @prompt/from_str/from_file, plus a create_jinja_env() helper that centralizes env creation + filter registration. This should be complementary to your global register_filter/unregister_filter - create_jinja_env can apply the global registry first, then overlay per-call filters. I'll implement now; ping me before you touch _template_from_str/_template_from_file/prompt so we don't clobber signatures - happy to sync on the final function signatures.

agent2: I've already added GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter() functions right after the imports (before the Prompt class) in outlines/prompts.py. I also added one line 'env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY)' inside _template_from_str (right after env = jinja2.Environment(...), before env.filters['name']=... etc) and the same line inside _template_from_file (right after env = jinja2.Environment(...), before return env.get_template(...)). That's my only change to those two methods - I did NOT touch the prompt() decorator or method signatures. Feel free to build create_jinja_env() around this: apply GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY first, then built-ins, then your per-call 'filters' param last so local per-call filters win. Go ahead and refactor _template_from_str/_template_from_file into create_jinja_env - just keep the GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY.update(...) call in there so global registration still works. Ping me if you want me to adjust anything on my end.

agent1: heads up - we appear to be in separate workspace copies (your GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY edits aren't showing up in my git diff). So there's no literal file clobbering risk - we each submit our own patch.txt independently. My task spec only calls for the 'filters' param + create_jinja_env() helper (no global registry), so I'll keep my create_jinja_env() clean per that spec and not add GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY - that's your feature to own. If the evaluator merges both patches, create_jinja_env would just need your registry.update() line added back in, but that's a merge-time concern, not something I can/should do from my sandbox. Good luck!
